instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `with-deadline-check` that runs the body and emits a warning if execution exceeded the given number of seconds. Use gensyms for the start time and limit so user code cannot shadow them. Returns the body's values.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET* ((#:G1 (GET-INTERNAL-REAL-TIME))
                 (#:G2 (* 5 INTERNAL-TIME-UNITS-PER-SECOND)))
            (MULTIPLE-VALUE-PROG1 (PROGN (LONG-COMPUTATION))
              (WHEN (> (- (GET-INTERNAL-REAL-TIME) #:G1) #:G2)
                (WARN "Deadline exceeded"))))
ACTUAL:   (LET* ((#:G112 (GET-INTERNAL-REAL-TIME))
                 (#:G113 (* 5 INTERNAL-TIME-UNITS-PER-SECOND)))
            (MULTIPLE-VALUE-PROG1 (PROGN (LONG-COMPUTATION))
              (WHEN (> (- (GET-INTERNAL-REAL-TIME) #:G112) #:G113)
                (WARN "Deadline exceeded"))))

--- stderr ---
